summary: DHH announces the Basecamp manifesto, a set of guiding beliefs for developing and running the application. He connects these beliefs to Scandinavian values and participatory design principles from his informatics background. The post serves partly as promotion for the Building of Basecamp workshop series, noting previous workshops sold out quickly. It frames Basecamp's product decisions as flowing from an explicit set of convictions about how software should work.
key_points: Basecamp's product decisions are guided by an explicit manifesto of beliefs about software development
DHH draws a connection between Basecamp's philosophy and Scandinavian participatory design values
The manifesto explains the reasoning behind Basecamp's structure, features, and deliberate constraints
37signals was actively teaching their methodology through sold-out workshops
money_quotes: It's a behind the scenes of our minds.
Being a student of informatics, I'm particularly proud to see so many Scandinavian values reflected.
It explains many of the decisions visible in the structure, features, and constraints of Basecamp.
It's about why we started to develop Basecamp and why we continue to push on.
The high priests of participatory design would be proud.
key_insight: Great software products should be built on an explicit set of beliefs that guide every decision about structure, features, and constraints.
